The '''20th Century Fox Fanfare''' is a musical piece played to accompany the [[20th Century Studios|20th Century Fox]] logo when it was displayed during the opening of the movie studio's feature films. The fanfare has been played before the first six ''[[Star Wars]]'' films, appearing in each episode of the [[Original trilogy|original]] and [[Prequel trilogy|prequel]] trilogies.

==History==
The 20th Century Fox fanfare was composed in [[1930s|1933]] by [[Wikipedia:Alfred Newman (composer)|Alfred Newman]], a longtime head of Fox's music department from 1940 to 1960. In [[1950s|1953]], an extended version was created for CinemaScope films, and debuted on the film ''River of No Return''.

In the mid-to-late 1970s, the 20th Century Fox logo had all but been phased out. However, [[George Lucas]] enjoyed the logo and Alfred Newman music so much that he insisted it be used for his ''[[Star Wars]]'' films. The fanfare and logo have, since then, enjoyed a rebirth in usage.

When [[John Williams]] signed onto the ''Star Wars'' project, one of his first moves was to compose the [[Star Wars Main Title|Main Title]] to [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] in the same key as the 20th Century Fox fanfare. He has said before that it was truly meant as another extension of the fanfare, and it has since been adopted by ''Star Wars'' film score buffs as part and parcel of the scores to ''Star Wars''.

When [[Sony]] released the [[original trilogy]] [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]] scores, and the [[Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace (soundtrack)|Ultimate Edition]] score to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]], each set included a recording of the 20th Century Fox fanfare.

When the films were released digitally in [[April]] [[2015]] by [[The Walt Disney Company]], the Fox opening, as well as the fanfare, were absent, disappointing some fans.<ref name="nofanfare">{{WebCite|url=http://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/star-wars/11528213/Why-people-are-so-angry-about-digital-Star-Wars.html|text=Why people are so angry about digital Star Wars|author=Alice Vincent|date=2015-04-10|work=[[Wikipedia:The Daily Telegraph|''The Telegraph'']]|archivedate=20170504005933}}</ref> The Fox logo and fanfare was subsequently reinstated for the [[Disney+]] and Ultra HD Blu-ray release.<ref name="Disney+">{{WebCite|url=https://www.slashfilm.com/570402/star-wars-disney-changes-fox-fanfare-logo|text=Disney+ Changes Original 'Star Wars' Trilogy To Include Classic Fox Fanfare And Newly Tweaked Han/Greedo Confrontation|author=Hoai-Tran Bui|date=2019-11-12|work=[[Wikipedia:/Film|''/Film'']]|archivedate=20220102100712}}</ref>
